metadata.name: chainside-btcpy
metadata.summary: A Python3 SegWit-compliant library which provides tools to handle Bitcoin data structures in a simple fashion.

methods.name: btcpy.structs.hd.ExtendedKey.derive
documentation:
---
param path: |-
  a path like "m/44'/0'/1'/0/10" if deriving from a master key,
  or a relative path like "./0/10"
return: |-
  the derived ExtendedPublicKey if deriving from an ExtendedPublicKey,
  the derived ExtendedPrivateKey if deriving from an ExtendedPrivateKey
---

methods.name: btcpy.lib.parsing.Parser.__rshift__
documentation:
Moves the parser forward of `bytes_` bytes. This consumes the first
`bytes_` bytes of the string, and returns them
\:param [bytes]: number of bytes to shift the pointer
\:return: part of the string consumed

methods.name: btcpy.lib.parsing.TransactionParser._txins_data
documentation:
---
return: a couple (segwit, txins) where `segwit` is a boolean
---

telling whether the transaction has a segwit format

methods.name: btcpy.structs.transaction.SegWitTransaction.from_json
documentation: @classmethod
def unhexlify(cls, string):
tx = Transaction.unhexlify(string)
return SegWitTransaction(tx.version, tx.ins, tx.outs, tx.locktime)

methods.name: btcpy.structs.transaction.SegWitTransaction.size
documentation: the following properties must be redefined,
it does not work if one calls them on the inner transaction
the only difference with what is defined in Transaction
is that here _base_size() extracts the inner transaction's base_size

modules.name: btcpy.lib.base58
documentation:
Base58 encoding

Implementations of Base58 and Base58Check endcodings that are compatible
with the bitcoin network.

documentation: Encode a string using Base58

documentation: Decode a Base58 encoded string

functions.name: btcpy.lib.base58.b58encode_check
documentation: Encode a string using Base58 with a 4 character checksum

functions.name: btcpy.lib.base58.b58decode_check
documentation: Decode and verify the checksum of a Base58 encoded string

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.StackData

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.StackData.__init__
documentation:
---
param data: a bytearray of stack data
param push_op: a bytearray representing a push operation
---

Represents data pushed on the stack. This class has commodity methods to get the length in bytes of the data
and the push operation needed in a script to push those data on the stack
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.BaseScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.BaseScript.from_json
documentation: noinspection PyMethodOverriding
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.ScriptPubKey

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.ScriptPubKey.is_standard
documentation: Subclasses which have standard types should reimplement this method

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.ScriptPubKey.address
documentation:
Subclasses which have a meaningful concept of address should reimplement this. For the moment
we consider to have a meaningful address only for the following types: P2pkh, P2sh, P2wpkh, P2wsh

Subclasses must either redefine `template` or reimplement their own `verify()` static method.
They also must implement the `type` property.
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2pkhScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2pkhScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param param: can be either of type `Script`, `PublicKey`, `Address` or `bytearray`.
---

In the first case the script is verified and the public key hash is extracted.
In the second case the script is built hashing the public key.
In the third case, the script is built extracting the pubkeyhash from the address
In the fourth case, the script is built from the public key hash (represented by the bytearray)
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2shScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2shScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param param: can be either of type `Script`, `ScriptPubKey`, `Address` or `bytearray`.
---

In the first case, the script is verified and the hash of the redeem script is extracted.
In the second case, the script is built by hashing the redeem script.
In the third case, the script is built by base58 decoding the address
In the fourth case, the script is built from the script hash (represented by the bytearray)
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2wshV0Script
documentation: noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2pkScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.P2pkScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param param: can be either of type `Script` or `PublicKey`.
---

In the first case it is verified and the public key is extracted.
In the second case the script is built from the public key
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.NulldataScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.NulldataScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param param: can be either of type `Script` or `StackData`.
---

In the first case the script is verified and the data is extracted.
In the second case the script is built from the data provided.
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.MultisigScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.MultisigScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: if one arg is provided that is interpreted as a precompiled script which
  needs
---

verification to see if it belongs to this type. Once verification is done, `m`, a list of pubkeys
and `n` are extracted and saved.
If more than one arg is provided, we assume that the parameters are `m, pubkey1, ..., pubkeyn, n`.
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.IfElseScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.IfElseScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: if one arg is provided, it is interpreted as a script, which is in turn
---

verified and if-branch and else-branch are extracted. If two args are provided they
are supposed to be the if-branch and else-branch. In this case the script is created
from these data
noinspection PyMissingConstructor
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.AbsoluteTimelockScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.AbsoluteTimelockScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: if one arg is provided it is interpreted as a script, which is in turn
---

verified and `locktime` and `locked_script` are extracted. If two args are provided,
they are interpreted as `locktime` and `locked_script` respectively, the script is
then generated from these params
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

classes.name: btcpy.structs.script.RelativeTimelockScript

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.RelativeTimelockScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: if one arg is provided, it is interpreted as a script, which is in turn
---

verified and `sequence` and `locked_script` are extracted. If two args are provided,
they are interpreted as `sequence` and `locked_script` respectively, the script is
then generated from these params
noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

methods.name: btcpy.structs.script.HashlockScript.__init__
documentation:
---
param args: if one arg is provided, it is interpreted as a script, which is in turn
---

verified and `hash` and `locked_script` are extracted. If two args are provided,
they are interpreted as `hash` and `locked_script` respectively, the script is
then generated from these params

documentation: can't be an internal ScriptPubKey format

class_variables.name: btcpy.structs.script.ScriptBuilder.not_allowed_redeem
